CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100));
CREATE TABLE logs (log_id INT PRIMARY KEY, user_id INT, action VARCHAR(100), timestamp TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO users (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Alice');
INSERT INTO users (id, name) VALUES (2, 'Bob');
INSERT INTO logs (log_id, user_id, action, timestamp) VALUES (1, 1, 'login', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO logs (log_id, user_id, action, timestamp) VALUES (2, 2, 'logout', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT * FROM users WHERE id IN (SELECT user_id FROM logs WHERE action = 'login');
SELECT u.name FROM users u JOIN logs l ON u.id = l.user_id WHERE l.action = 'logout' AND l.timestamp BETWEEN NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour' AND NOW();
SELECT u.name, COUNT(l.action) FROM users u LEFT JOIN logs l ON u.id = l.user_id GROUP BY u.id HAVING COUNT(l.action) > 1;
trace_on ('user_log', 'failed_log');
SELECT * FROM users WHERE name LIKE '%A%';
UPDATE users SET name = 'Alice Smith' WHERE id = 1;
DELETE FROM logs WHERE timestamp < NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days';
SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = (SELECT user_id FROM logs ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 1);
CREATE TABLE transactions (trans_id INT PRIMARY KEY, user_id INT, amount DECIMAL(10, 2), created_at TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO transactions (trans_id, user_id, amount, created_at) VALUES (1, 1, 100.00, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO transactions (trans_id, user_id, amount, created_at) VALUES (2, 2, 200.00, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT SUM(amount) FROM transactions WHERE user_id = 1 GROUP BY user_id HAVING SUM(amount) > 100;
SELECT * FROM users u JOIN transactions t ON u.id = t.user_id WHERE t.amount > 150 ORDER BY t.created_at DESC;
trace_on ('transactions', 'failed_transaction_log');
